What is Earn2Trade?

Earn2Trade is an education-focused futures prop firm founded in 2016 and based in Orlando, Florida. The company offers structured trading evaluation programs that combine virtual trading competitions with formal education, allowing aspiring futures traders to prove their skills and earn access to funded accounts up to $400K through their broker partner, Helios Trading Partners. With a 4.0/5 rating and pricing starting at $99 per month for the Gauntlet Mini program, Earn2Trade is best suited for disciplined intraday futures traders who value structured learning alongside performance-based funding. The platform is strictly futures-focused, making it ideal for traders who want to specialize in this market without distractions from stocks, options, or crypto spot trading.

How to Create Your Earn2Trade Account

Creating an Earn2Trade account takes approximately 10-15 minutes and requires basic information and identity verification. Here's what you'll need:

  • Email address and password: Create a unique, strong password for your account security.
  • Personal information: Full name, date of birth, address, and phone number for identity verification.
  • Trading experience: Earn2Trade asks about your background—they accept traders at all experience levels, but this data helps them tailor resources to your needs.
  • Payment method: Credit card or bank account for monthly subscription fees. Earn2Trade charges recurring monthly fees (no annual discounts available).
  • Identity verification: You'll need to upload a government-issued ID (passport or driver's license). This process typically verifies within 24 hours.

Once your identity is verified, you'll immediately gain access to the dashboard and can choose which evaluation program to join. There's no lengthy approval process—you pay the monthly fee and start your evaluation period that same day.

Setting Up Earn2Trade for the First Time

Dashboard Navigation: When you log in, you'll see your account balance, current evaluation progress, and trading statistics prominently displayed. The main navigation includes "Evaluation," "Education," "Account Settings," and "Community" tabs. Familiarize yourself with where everything lives before your first trade.

Broker Platform Selection: Earn2Trade requires you to trade on approved platforms only—currently NinjaTrader or R|Trader. These aren't optional; the platform restriction exists to ensure consistent data collection and compliance. If you don't already have one of these platforms, download and install it before your evaluation period begins. Both platforms integrate with your Earn2Trade account through your broker credentials.

Configure Risk Settings: Under Account Settings, set your daily loss limit and maximum loss tolerance. Earn2Trade enforces strict risk management rules (fixed drawdown limits based on your account tier), and the platform will pause trading if you hit these thresholds. Review these limits carefully—exceeding them ends your evaluation immediately, regardless of profit.

Link Your Broker Account: Earn2Trade uses Helios Trading Partners as the broker behind funded accounts. During setup, you'll authorize the connection between your trading platform and Earn2Trade's backend. This allows real-time profit/loss tracking and ensures all trades are recorded accurately for evaluation purposes.

Enable Notifications: Turn on email and in-platform notifications for trade confirmations, daily P&L summaries, and risk alerts. These notifications help you stay aware of your evaluation progress and prevent accidental rule violations.

Essential Features You Should Know

Paper Trading and Evaluation: All Earn2Trade programs start with paper trading (simulated trading with no real money). The Gauntlet Mini requires you to meet profit targets and stay within drawdown limits over a 10-day minimum evaluation period. This is one of the shortest evaluation windows in the industry and makes Earn2Trade attractive for traders who want quick feedback. Your evaluation trades are recorded on a virtual account, and you'll see real-time P&L updates as you trade.

Performance Analytics: Earn2Trade's dashboard shows detailed trade statistics including win rate, average profit per trade, largest winning and losing trades, and daily/weekly/monthly P&L charts. These analytics help you identify patterns in your trading behavior and spot weakness areas during evaluation. Unlike some prop firms that hide analytics behind complex reports, Earn2Trade displays everything clearly on the main dashboard so you can track progress daily.

Risk Management Tools: Built-in risk controls enforce hard stops on daily losses and maximum drawdown. The platform automatically halts your account if you exceed these limits, preventing catastrophic losses. For example, a $25K Gauntlet Mini account has specific daily loss and total drawdown limits—exceeding either one ends your evaluation. These constraints force disciplined risk management from day one and are non-negotiable.

Broker Integration: Earn2Trade integrates directly with NinjaTrader and R|Trader, automatically syncing every trade you place. You don't manually enter trades or submit screenshots; the integration pulls data in real-time. This eliminates paperwork and human error in trade reporting and ensures your evaluation results are accurate and auditable.

Social Features and Community: The platform includes a community forum where traders share strategies, ask questions, and discuss market conditions. You can view some other traders' results (anonymously) to benchmark your performance. The social element is useful for motivation and learning, though it can also lead to overconfidence if you compare yourself to outlier performers.

Education Library: Earn2Trade's education-first approach is evident in the built-in learning materials. The Trader Career Path tier ($199/mo) includes structured modules on futures trading, market fundamentals, and risk management. The Gauntlet Mini tiers ($99–$149/mo) have access to educational resources but less structured curriculum. Video lessons, trading journals, and strategy guides are available within the platform.

Earn2Trade Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Gauntlet Mini $25K ($99/mo): This is Earn2Trade's entry-level program. You evaluate on a $25K paper account and, if you pass, receive access to a $25K funded account with an 80/20 profit split (you keep 80%). Monthly fees add up quickly ($99 × months before passing), so only choose this if you're confident about your skills or treating the cost as education. The 10-day minimum evaluation is manageable, but many traders fail and must restart, doubling costs.

Gauntlet Mini $50K ($149/mo): This tier doubles your evaluation account size to $50K, giving you more flexibility in position sizing. The profit split remains 80/20. It costs $50 more per month than the $25K tier but doesn't meaningfully improve your odds of passing—it simply gives you a larger account to trade if you succeed. Choose this if you prefer larger position sizes or are experienced enough to confidently scale your account.

Trader Career Path ($199/mo): This is Earn2Trade's premium program and includes comprehensive education alongside evaluation. It's structured as a career progression path rather than a one-time evaluation, with clear steps from learning to evaluation to funding. The monthly cost is highest, but you get structured curriculum. This tier is ideal for newer traders willing to invest in education before evaluation, or traders who value a clear roadmap over jumping straight into evaluation.

No Free Tier Available: Earn2Trade doesn't offer free paper trading or a trial period. You must pay the monthly fee to access any program. There's no way to test the platform before committing financially. Budget accordingly—even if you pass on your first try, you're investing at least $99–$199 per month.

Account Scaling After Funding: If you pass evaluation and receive a funded account, you can scale from $25K up to $400K based on consistent profitability. Each scaling tier has its own requirements and time commitments, but the potential for larger accounts makes the initial monthly investment worthwhile for successful traders.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Earn2Trade

Master Your Platform Before Your Evaluation Starts: Spend 3–5 days on NinjaTrader or R|Trader before paying for an evaluation. Buttons, shortcuts, and order types should be automatic in your muscle memory. During evaluation, fumbling with platform controls costs you money and trading opportunities. The platform learning curve is steep, but it's not Earn2Trade's problem to solve once you begin.

Know Your Daily and Total Drawdown Limits Exactly: Each tier has specific limits. Write them down or set phone reminders. Many traders violate these limits unknowingly because they weren't paying attention. Earn2Trade enforces these rules strictly—there's no grace period or appeal. One careless day exceeding the limit ends your evaluation and wastes your monthly fee.

Track Your Evaluation Progress Daily: Don't wait until day 9 of 10 to check if you're on pace to pass. Log into your dashboard every day and verify you're hitting profit targets while staying within drawdown limits. Waiting until the last moment leaves no time to adjust if you're falling short.

Use the Education Materials for Your Specific Trading Style: Earn2Trade's educational content is generalized—it covers broad futures principles but not your exact strategy. However, the risk management and journal frameworks are universally applicable. Spend time with these resources even if the specific trading examples don't match your approach. The discipline habits they teach transfer directly to evaluation.

Test Your Strategy in Paper Trading Outside of Evaluation: Before paying for your official evaluation, trade the same strategy on the same platform using your own paper account for 2–3 weeks. Prove to yourself that your edge exists in real market conditions with real-time price action. This costs nothing and saves you $99–$199 if you discover your strategy doesn't work.

Join the Community Forum and Learn from Others' Failures: Traders frequently post about what caused them to fail evaluation (hitting drawdown limits on one bad day, overtrading, ignoring signals, etc.). These case studies are valuable. Don't fall into the same traps others documented publicly.

Factor in Multiple Attempts in Your Budget: Only 8.89% of evaluation candidates pass on their first try. If you attempt evaluation and fail, you'll need to pay the monthly fee again to restart. Budget for 2–3 attempts realistically. If passing evaluation costs $300–$600 before success, your break-even point on the 80/20 profit split becomes higher.

Common Earn2Trade Issues and How to Fix Them

Platform Connection Failures: Occasionally, the integration between Earn2Trade and NinjaTrader/R|Trader drops, and trades aren't syncing. If your platform shows a trade but Earn2Trade's dashboard doesn't reflect it, restart both applications and check your broker login credentials. If the problem persists after 30 minutes, contact Earn2Trade support immediately—they respond within a few hours and can manually sync missed trades if needed. Don't assume a trade didn't happen; verify it on Earn2Trade's platform before opening another position.

Accidental Rule Violation on the Last Day: You hit your daily loss limit on day 9 of 10, thinking you still had room. Check your account settings before every trading session to confirm your remaining daily loss buffer. Earn2Trade provides this information clearly, but traders often misread or forget limits. One $15K daily loss limit isn't the same as $5K—verify this before trading.

Withdrawal and Payout Delays: After passing evaluation and generating profits, payouts process within 5–10 business days. Some traders panic if they don't see funds immediately. This is normal. Earn2Trade offers both bank transfers and crypto payouts (Bitcoin, Ethereum, etc.)—select your preferred method in Account Settings to avoid payment method mismatches. If you've waited over 10 business days, contact support with proof of your profit withdrawal request.

Evaluation Period Confusion Between Tiers: Gauntlet Mini has a 10-day minimum evaluation, but some traders think they can pass in fewer days if they hit targets early. Clarification: you must complete the full evaluation window (at least 10 days) even if you hit profit targets on day 3. The evaluation period is a time requirement, not just a profit requirement. Review your specific tier's rules before starting.

Is Earn2Trade Worth It? Our Verdict

Earn2Trade is worth it for disciplined futures traders who value education alongside evaluation and can afford multiple attempts if needed. The 4.0/5 rating reflects consistently strong customer support, quick 10-day minimum evaluations, and genuine funding opportunities—94.77% of funded traders are on the platform actively, meaning payouts are real. However, the 80/20 profit split underperforms competitors like Topstep (90/10) and Apex Trader Funding (100% first $25K), and the 8.89% pass rate means most traders will spend $300+ before success. The futures-only focus eliminates traders who want stock or options trading, and monthly fees with no annual discount make failed attempts expensive. If you're committed to futures trading, have 2–3 months of evaluation fees saved up, and want structured education alongside evaluation, Earn2Trade delivers value—especially the Trader Career Path tier. If you want quick funding with maximum profit keep, look elsewhere.

For a detailed breakdown of all features and trader experiences, see our full Earn2Trade review and comparisons with Earn2Trade vs Topstep and Earn2Trade vs Apex Trader Funding.
